content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be structured as a nested hierarchy of meta-agents, where each sub-agent operates as a curiosity-driven active learner that selectively surfaces only high-uncertainty, high-information stored experiences to the core agent, rather than dumping all retrieved memory context. This nested curious sub-agent architecture solves the core problem of memory context overload that plagues modern long-horizon agent design.
